# The Kitchen Regulator

Mirel kept the Brambled Inn the way her mother had: ledger square, kitchen
warm, questions few. When the kitchen regulator began losing an hour a day,
she mentioned it to no one — and found Boxwell at the workbench anyway,
gears laid out on a clean cloth, asking for nothing but lamplight.

He repaired it without asking payment. From that evening, the innkeeper
Mirel trusted Boxwell's discretion entirely, and kept the corner room made
up for him whether he came or not.
